question: A process uses an embedded subprocess, a call activity, and an event subprocess. A team wants to know which of these subprocess types are supported by the process instance migration tool. What is the answer?

options.text: Embedded subprocesses, call activities, and event subprocesses are all supported.

a.text: Migration supports more than just embedded subprocesses; call activities and event subprocesses are also listed as supported.

b.text: The supported BPMN elements section explicitly lists embedded subprocesses, call activities, and event subprocesses as supported subprocess types for migration.

c.text: Call activities are supported, but so are embedded subprocesses and event subprocesses.

d.text: Event subprocesses are supported, but so are embedded subprocesses and call activities.

explanation: The supported BPMN elements for migration include three subprocess types: embedded subprocesses, call activities, and event subprocesses. All three may appear in process instances that the migration tool handles.
